story_text: I have now been ring the surgery for the last two weeks to get an appointment,when I ring at eight in the morning phones are engaged if I finally get through the appointments have all gone.this surgery is an absolute joke I now have to go up to the local hospital to get sorted it could of been done at the surgery.more chance of raising the Titanic in the local Canal than getting an appointment at this surgery
resp_text:
Thank you for your feedback. I am sorry that you were not able to get an appointment when you wanted one. Because of the extra demands now being placed on General Practice, it is not always possible to get an appointment at the day and time however this information may assist you.
We book over 25% of the appointments in advance so you don't have to wait until the day to book. We have 5 incoming lines to the surgey and most patients all try to call at the same time so unfortuantely there will be times when the line is engaged, we just ask that you keep trying. We undertook a recent capacity and demand exercise to look at when patients call and are working to change the phone system to accommodate this and also ask patients who do not need an appointment to call after 10am to allow those that do to get through.
If you have access to a computer, please consider getting a user name and password from the surgery which will enable you to book online for the pre bookable appointments and this will give you a choice of days and times. Those patients that have these have fedback how great it is to book for later in the week or the the following week, even when the surgery is closed for answering the telephone. 2 forms of ID will be needed along with proof of address and an application form needs to be completed which we hold in reception. This also gives access to repeat prescription ordering and summary records or you can apply for access to see the coded entries in your medical record.
You are welcome to speak to the reception team, the team leader or myself about your experience and we will happy look at the individual case and review what happened on the day to prevent you form attending the hospital with something that you rightly point out could have been dealt with here.
As most comments are anonymous, we have not been able to identlfy you to disucss. We look forward to hearing from you

title_text: They work with you one to one

story_text:
As I am writing this my wife has now been on the phone for over an 90 minutes trying to speak to someone to get pain relief as she is undergoing cancer treatment. She has been number 2 in the queue for over 25 minutes! We have been with this practice for over 28 years and this practice has got worse over the years to the point where we are going to change practices. It has been going downhill for years but the introduction of the new anima app has been a total disaster. They know it's not working but persist with it. I pity the older people who need an appointment. I have been in the waiting room where they have actually recommend you go to A & E.
If you are thinking of joining this practice - avoid!
resp_text: Thank you for taking the time to post your review here on the NHS website. We welcome any and all comments from our patients and would therefore value the opportunity to discuss this with you directly; by sharing your experience with us, we can look to improve our service for all our patients. If this is something you would like to do, please contact your practice directly and ask to speak with the Duty Manager, telling them this is in response to a comment posted to the NHS website.

story_text: We received texts to book blood test appointments in Hamerton hospital. Blood tests usually need a referral indicating what tests to be performed, and then you go to the test centers walk-in. Never heard before about such appointments. When tried to call the GP, was 20 minutes on hold and still 5th in queue. Not wanting to stay there for days, I quitted! The worst service ever!
resp_text: Dear Anonymous. Unfortunately have any patient details for you I can not look into your record. You are quite right that if a patient has been requested to go for a blood test at Homerton the patient will need a blood test form, was the form attached to your text message? Was you asked to come to the practice to collect the form?I'm sorry that you was on hold for 20 minutes but we have recently improved our telephone system that allows our patients to leave the queue, (put the phone down) and they will be automatically called back once a receptionist is available. Please contact the surgery so that we may investigate this.

story_text: These doctors lack empathy towards their patients. I have never been to a place where the doctors are so cold and robotic towards you. Worst practice ever!!!
resp_text: We are sorry to hear that your experience at our surgery did not meet your expectations, thank you for taking the time to contact us. We have recently undertaken one of our regular communication skills update courses with our clinical staff, and hope that your experience will change in future. We are working closely with our patient panel and would like to meet with you and them together to understand your concerns and work together on areas which have caused you difficulties. Please contact our patient panel if you are happy to meet. Kind regards Dr Joanne Watt Dear Patient We write to you as representatives of the Patients Panel of the Patients Participation Group (PPG) at the Great Oakley Medical Centre. The PPG can be found here: http://www.greatoakleymedicalcentre.co.uk/ppg.aspx. The PPG is run by patients and its mandate is to improve communication with patients. The 'Patients Panel' is a subgroup of the PPG and members of this panel contact patients who have specific issues to report (such as with the NHS Choices postings). We would be happy to initiate a discussion with you on the issues you raised in your posting of the 4th of October 2016, where you mention the surgery is the 'worst practice ever'. Thanks so much for letting us know how we may contact you. Best Regards Patients Panel

story_text:
Anything else?
I visited the minor injuries unit on advice from my GP. I hurt the heel of my foot in a fall and hadn't been able to walk on it for five days at this point. I had tried to let it heal first with rest and anti-inflammatory medicine as I didn't want to go to the hospital, but it was only getting worse.   I don't mind waiting as it's uncontrollable but rude staff is another issue. I was assessed by a nurse first who I felt wasn't listening to what I was telling her. She had automatically assumed that I thought my foot was broken, which I knew wasn't.   I then was shoved in to get an xray and once the nurse finished I was told to wait on the table. A few minutes later another nurse came in barking at me to get out and wait in the hall.   I could then hear a nurse talking about me to another nurse. This nurse then took me to a desk to tell me what I already knew, that in fact my bone was not broken and it was more than likely a sprain. She told me to ice and rest it (which is what I had been doing for the past five days). I told her that due to the location of my work it was impossible for me to rest it and that it's painful to walk on. She didn't listen to me but instead told me 'that life must go on' and that was that.  I can honestly say I burst in to tears when I left because she had been to horrible to me, not at all empathetic or understanding. I felt that the staff had no respect for me and no interest in helping me. If they had I now would not have a sore ankle or knee on my other leg from supporting my weight incorrectly.   I do not pay taxes for poor care or be treated with such little respect.
resp_text: We were very sorry to read these comments, and wish to express our sincere apologies to this person.    Rudeness is never acceptable, so that we are at a loss to explain why this patient had such a negative experience of our service.    As the comments are anonymous, and make no reference to the date of attendance, we are unable to investigate this further. However, the Minor Injuries staff have been made aware of this review and it is hoped they will be more mindful of the feelings of others in future.    As a rule, we endeavour to treat our visitors with kindness and respect, tailoring our care to the needs of the individual. I am sorry to hear that we fell so short of the mark on this occasion.

title_text: Visiting my father  inpatient for 5 weeks...
story_text: As a family we were very impressed and feel very grateful for the level of support my father received from staff at Duchy Court. He was treated with care and respect from the time of his admission following a very traumatic introduction to in patient services via another hospital a and e, through his recovery, and the level of liaison with community and more local services before  his discharge was very thorough. In general both he and we were treated with a very high level of compassion and care and the consultant, nursing staff and in particular the nursing assistants have played a very significant role in his recovery.     The ward rounds were held every week as agreed and we were consulted about attending and times were agreed with us, we found these informative and supportive. The general environment was very clean and my father reported that the food was very good.      I have worked in other units in the Greater Manchester area as a professional and feel that Duchy Court was exemplary in care in comparison and we remain ever grateful to all the staff for their care during a difficult time,
resp_text: I am delighted to read these very positive comments and will pass these onto staff who work at Duchy Court so they can take pleasure in knowing how patients and carers view the services offered there.    I am very thankful to you for taking the time to share your experience as it enables us to understand what matters to patients and carers and whether we are doing it right.      All the very best    Shahid Islam  Patient Experience Manager

story_text:

title_text: By Far the Best Practice in Warwickshire
story_text: St Wulfstan is aptly named - It must be protected by Saints, and it is occupied by saintly receptionists, doctors and nurses. The best way to describe it is as a safe, welcoming, comforting and serene practice. Adjectives that I have never used for any previous practice that I've attended in this region. the appointments system runs like clock work. The nurses are exceptionally well trained, always professional helpful, kind and generous with their support. Your doctor will remember you, and they will show you care and attention - never rushed, never hasty, always excellent. I moved into this area in 2015 (from Leamington Spa) and my whole life changed thanks to this wonderful practice which has shown me how great medical care can be.
resp_text: Thank you so much for such glowing comments, Katia. Everyone at St Wulfstan tries really hard to provide the best service they can and the whole team were delighted to hear how happy you have been with our care. nLindsay Ward, Practice Manager

story_text:
My dad recently underwent a quadruple heart by-pass and was admitted to ICU post-op.  
Whilst my dad was ventilated we as a family decided not to visit him at this point. During this time when we called the ward the nurses ensured we were kept fully up to date. The nurses were very kind and compassionate and offered a lot of reassurance to us during this worrying time.  
I am aware of how busy the nurses are, taking care of such sick patients, but they always took the time to speak to us on the phone and offer their expertise knowledge to the family.  
In a time where my dad was very unwell the nurses made us feel like he was getting very well looked after. Also a special thanks to Sarah, Jo and Veejay who were outstanding and a credit to the ward.  
My dad is now home and is recovering well. Thanks again to all the staff and also to the surgeons who carried out this amazing surgery. We will be forever grateful.
resp_text: Dear Kilodr73,  rnThank you for taking the time to post on Care Opinion regarding your dad’s recent surgery. What a terrifying time this must have been for you all. I am so pleased to read that he is home and recovering well. Please pass on my well wishes to him.  rnPlease be assured that I have shared your kind compliments with the ICU managers. The nursing team will be delighted to know that the family felt very reassured by the phone calls with them and how they were caring for your dad. I will especially pass your thanks onto Sarah, Jo and Veejay.  rnThank you again,  rnKind regards

title_text: The treatment l have had is disappointing
story_text:
I had a test a year last May to be told I had lost the hearing nearly in one ear and would get a follow up to decide what they were going to do and continual phone calls - no appointment!  
Eventually I got one for Lincoln Hospital recently, only after a round trip of a 100 miles to be told I needed another hearing test as it was so long ago.  
Why could I have not been notified before making the journey to Lincoln, which was absolutely a waste of time?  
Again I have been promised it will be within 6 weeks. l was told the same last year, my hearing is a lot worse now.  
The treatment l have had is disappointing
resp_text:
Dear tom571  
We are sorry you feel disappointed with our service and you had to wait a long time for a review appointment from last year. If we had your details to hand we would be able to look into this specifically for you and provide a more tailored response. We do have a large volume of patients awaiting an appointment with our service and this does unfortunately cause delays as we are a relatively small service covering the county of Lincolnshire.  
You do not state whether you had an examination during your appointment at Lincoln County recently; we find it is best for a face to face appointment as the clinical staff are able to ascertain if there is anything else they need to be aware of, particularly as you had been waiting a long time for a review.  
We hope you receive your hearing test appointment as soon as possible and if you would like to contact a member of our team we will be able to check into this for you with our audiology colleagues.  
We are happy for you to contact the service manager direct to help you with this.  
Kind regards  
Andrea  
01205 445746/01522 307400 - andrea.herd@ulh.nhs.uk

title_text: Out of hours care from out Strabane
story_text: I have been suffering with a severe ear infection for the last few days. The antibiotics and pain relief I was on wasn't hitting the pain and I was in incredible agony. I rang out of hours in Strabane and was rang back within 5mins to come down straight away. I was quite visibly upset when I got there and tried my best to keep it in. I was seen within 10-15mins by a nurse called Amy. She seen how upset I was and how much pain I was in and couldn't do enough to help. She was caring, kind, polite and so friendly. I know that's what you expect in these situations but most times I have been in an out of hours practice.. they normally want rid of you quickly or refer you on to A&E. This was not the case this evening. I feel that Amy set the standard for all healthcare workers in her professionalism, knowledge and kindness. She gave me a clear going over and left no stone unturned. I left feeling so relieved, with everything I needed and finally was able to get the pain under control and get some sleep. Amy was quick and to the point and so compassionte to how awful I felt. I truly appreciated everything she did for me.
resp_text: Firstly, thank you for taking the time to share the details of your experience with the GP Out of Hours service.  rnWe were glad to hear that you had a positive experience of the service from the telephone triage to the appointment to be seen in the clinic. It was good to know that the Doctor you seen was able to assist you. We will pass on your kind words and appreciation so that she is aware you valued the attention and care she gave to you.  rnWe do hope that your health continues to improve.

story_text: Whenever I ring this surgery for appointments for myself or my family members I can NEVER get an appointment. They’re service is absolutely rubbish. Taking on more patients than they can cope with. I rang a couple months back for my son as he was covere ln a rash but they refused to give an appointment. Today I was suffering from severe back pain which I had for the past week and when I rang for an appointment they said come in to the walk in clinic. I took the time to go there with this pain and they said they can’t see me. After having a go at them I told them no one comes here for fun atheist give an appointment but they have no appointment system. That’s what the moody receptions had to say. Pathetic practice .
resp_text:
I am really sorry your experience was less than satisfactory.
we are constantly reviewing our appointment system to try and meet the needs of all of our patients.
With this in mind we actually introduced a sit and wait clinic on Monday and Thursday mornings to increase capacity however its very difficult to try and guess how many patients are going to walk in on a given day so the standard telephone script is that its walk in clinic we then tell the caller if we have a q in reception of people walking in and try to encourage patients to come to surgery asap as each of the drs see 26 patients once we reach 26 then we close the walk in and have to turn patients away.
In addition to the we have also added on line appointment booking which is open 24/7 so that patients can book online rather than calling in and finally we have advanced booking so everyday 50% of appointments are bookable in advance (2 weeks in advance) and the remaining appointments are booked on the day.
Having said all of the I totally understand just how frustrating this experience must have been for you.
In the future if you experience anything like this again please do ask to speak to myself Julie Davey Practice Manager or my senior receptionist Anisah - we do have the authority or override our booking system in situations such as this if we feel it appropriate.
I would welcome the opportunity to discuss this further with you when you next have a few minutes and passing by the surgery.

story_text: I have just returned from an appointment in you Neurophysiology Dept for tests for Carpel problems. My experience was superb. Such a lovely person who did these tests and seen on time, which in itself is a plus. Many thanks.

story_text:
I attended Day Surgery University Hospital Wishaw for an open reduction and internal fixation of the right humerus. The reception/admin staff who guided me to the waiting room were pleasant and extremely helpful. I found the Nursing staff in the department to be caring, empathic and highly professional. I felt at ease and trusted them entirely from the outset which was huge as I was terrified about having this surgery.  
The surgical team and all the theatre staff and anaesthetists involved in my care I will forever be grateful for your excellent care. I was discharged the same day with a 5 day care plan which guided me through the post op days in the comfort of my own home.Thank you x 1 million to these amazing healthcare providers feel extremely lucky to have had them.
resp_text:

Kind regards,  
Jenni

title_text: 12 days of calm, comfort and being looked after
story_text:
Turned up at Dame Carol House extremely anxious, nervous and scared of what to expect and the journey ahead.  
I was instantly greeted by a wonderful team of nurses and recovery workers who made me feel at ease instantly. I was shown my room which was well equipped, clean and comforting. What followed was 12 days of calm, comfort and being looked after and having any need addressed. The team here have been life savers, truly wonderful!I have left feeling confident about my recovery, knowing the team are always on the other end of the phone. They have set me up for success which I never thought I would be able to achieve. Thank you from the bottom of my heart for what you all have done!
resp_text: Hello Camm,  rnThank you for taking the time to leave a review for us here at Dame Carol.  rnI am pleased you felt at ease when you arrived.  rnI am glad you felt comfortable whilst staying here.  rnWe wish you all the luck for the future and thank you for your comments.  rnEmma Smith Bed Manager

It is wonderful to hear that Lindsey provided your son with the care he needed and that Annie was able to effectively communicate and follow up on your son’s treatment plan, ensuring that you felt supported throughout the process.  rnI will be happy to pass on your thanks to the team who I'm sure will be touched to hear your kind words.  rnThank you once again for your heartfelt appreciation. title_text: Thank you for helping us find our way
story_text:
We were referred to RHC with our newborn for a dental examination. Even with the time to prep and plan our journey, we were traveling to an unfamiliar location (with baby in the car seat for only the second time!) with the added stress we may need to put our little one through a procedure so early in her life.  
We were not provided an official letter from the pediatrician at the hospital, so we initially had a bit of back-and-forth between the reception and clinic at the RHC. Just wanted to drop a note to thank Joanne at reception and Julie and her colleagues for making our visit go as smoothly as possible. Joanne kindly took the time to contact the relevant clinic and guide us there for our “appointment without an appointment”, which helped reduce our stress levels at the thought of having to come back at a later date without having had our little one checked over.  
Julie and her colleagues answered every question we had before we had a chance to ask in relation to the dental referral for our little one, all while being kind and sympathetic to our situation. All staff at the RHC were lovely, and we’d like to thank all for making our visit stress free.
resp_text: Good Afternoon Hotelierx77  rnAs the Senior Charge Nurse of Outpatients RHC. I would personally like to thank you for leaving wonderful feedback on your recent visit to the dental department.  rnI was pleased to hear that all staff were able to help and make your visit with your baby daughter very pleasant.  rnJoanne and Julie will be informed of this wonderful feedback.  rnThank you once again.  rnMhairi Stirrat  rnSenior Charge Nurse  rnOutpatients RHC

story_text: Extremely delighted with the response time, appointments and staff at the centre.  Procedures were explained in detail with follow up written documentation to bring home.  Provided refreshment and snacks while waiting on both occasions. title_text: The understanding, kind team on this...
story_text:
What I liked
It was my daughter who after traumatic circumstances had to visit the Wilson Suite - Ward 34.  This could never be a pleasant experience, but the team of ladies on duty were absolutely fantastic.  Explained everything and dealt with very traumatic circumstances very calmly and kindley without being toosoft or patronising.  Owing to circumstances of visiting that ward, we were there for about 16 hours and they were not judgemental or harsh in any way.  The explanations of what was happening to my daughter throughout the process were given and I can honestly say it was an absolute pleasure to meet these amazing ladies who work extremely hard, long hours with total dedication.
What could be improved
Nothing
Anything else?
People are very quick to complain, but "thank you" is rarely said.  So, thank you very much to the staff involved.
resp_text: Your kind comments have been passed to the staff of the Wilson Suite - thank you.

story_text:
I was diagnosed with invasive breast cancer at Gartnavel Hospital, Glasgow, Breast Clinic in December 2021. I cannot rate the staff highly enough, from diagnoses through to surgery and care after surgery.  
After diagnosis, I could tell that the main goal was to get a plan in place as quickly as possible, but more importantly, that it was the right plan for my condition. The team certainly achieved this! There are so many in the team who have done a superb job in supporting, treating and caring for me during this difficult time in my life.  
I’d like to thank you all for doing such an amazing job. In particular, I’d like to express sincere gratitude to Miss Doughty, Mr Malyon, Ann, Kirsty and, last but not least, my wonderful Breast Care Nurse Jan. You’re all amazing!  
I may not remember all the factual details I was told throughout this time, but I’ll certainly remember how you made me feel.
resp_text: Dear vegajz78  rnThank you so much for taking the time to leave such lovely feedback in regards to your experience of using the Breast Clinic of the Gartnavel General Hospital.  rnI was sorry to hear you had been diagnosed with invasive breast cancer. This must have been incredibly difficult period for you and your family. I can only begin to imagine how worried you must have felt.  rnI was however relieved to hear of the support and advice you have been provided with throughout your journey.  rnIt was really heart warming to hear how you held all the staff you came across in such high regard and they were able to put a suitable plan in place quickly and efficiently to help with your treatment.  rnI will be delighted to pass on your thanks to Miss Doughty, Mr Malyon, Ann, Kirsty and, of course Breast Care Nurse Jan who I'm sure will all be touched to hear such glowing praise.  rnThanks again this means so much to all the staff involved.  rnKind regards  rnKevin

title_text: Great service during the COVID pandemic
story_text: I wanted to let you know how impressed I have been with the service and care that Hillview Medical Centre has provided for me over the past year despite being under pressure due to the pandemic. I have needed to have a number appointments with the Drs and nurse during this time and think that the service has actually improved in response to the situation! Most of my contact has been via email initially, so much easier than having to sit on the phone and the website is easy to navigate , these emails have been responded to very swiftly offering either an appointment or by a call from the Dr or nurse practitioner, sometimes within hours, normally within the same day. I am so grateful for the service and care that the practice have provided for me and would recommend you to anyone. Thank you!

story_text:

The mental health services refused to contact my GP to prevent the mistreatment.  
The mental health services made my position and my life worse, to the point where I feel victimised and mistreated by my GP. The complaints service has failed to take action on the information they received from myself, to help me to access appropriate and safe GP care. They have failed to respond to my complaint within appropriate timescales. They have failed to take action to protect myself and others from GP mistreatment.
resp_text: Dear Mcleary875  rnMy name is Jane and I am one of the Experience and Governance Leads for the Trust. I am sorry to learn that you have had such a negative experience and we were not able to resolve your complaint to your satisfaction.  rnAs you are unhappy with the complaint outcome and the way your complaint was handled, you may wish to raise this with the Parliamentary and Health Service Ombudsman (PHSO). The PHSO is independent of government and the NHS. The service is confidential and free. Please be aware that there is a time limit for making a complaint to the PHSO; this is generally within one year of you becoming aware of the issue, although there are exceptions.  rnIf you wish to discuss your case with the PHSO you can contact their helpline on 0345 015 4033, email phso.enquiries@ombudsman.org.uk. Further information about the PHSO is available at www.ombudsman.org.uk  rnKind regards  rnJane

Thank you for taking the time to leave feedback on your experience,
I am sorry to lear that you have found the situation stressful and the staff unhelpful.
We are aware that at the moment there is currently a longer wait than usual for a routine appointment, this is due to a combination of bank holidays and annual leave.
However, we do release a number of appointments on regular basis and if you do not already have on-line access it may be worth considering this as you can then easily see what appointments are available to you. More information on this can be found here http://www.nhs.uk/NHSEngland/AboutNHSservices/doctors/Pages/gp-online-services.aspx
If you have an urgent medical requirement we have same day appointments available and if these have been booked you can access the duty doctor who will call you back the same day and will review you over the telephone and may ask you to attend the surgery.
The receptionists are trained to give our test results and only provide details of those which have been authorised and reviewed by a doctor first.
You may be interested in joining our patient participation group which is a group of patients who work to introduce improvements to the practice.
Our next meeting is Thursday 13th April, 10am at the the practice if you would like to come along.
Thank you for your comments we use all feedback to help us to improve our services.
Dawn
Practice Manager

story_text:
I and my husband don't know anymore how we get help for our son. We used to ring a crisis team who would then arrange to see him and arrange an admission to hospital. Sometimes he was sectioned. He has schizophrenia .  
Now when we ring there is either a telephone line or no one answers at all. Just an answerphone. We need someone to pick up the phone if we call. It is not good enough to call us back hours later when we needed help at the time  
Is the crisis line really staffed by professionals? The advice we have received ranges from telling us there there is nothing we can do, to being simply dismissed as they say they are too busy. We need a clear practical plan but they don't seem to have one. We cant take him to A&E and telling us to call the police when he isn't a criminal is just wrong.  
I too have been told recently that the crisis line is not for emergencies. If this is correct , and they also don't come out , then what are they there for? My son is one of those the crisis team refuses to work with. We think there are quite a number of patients like this. The MH Trust cut the assertive outreach team he was once under but hasn't replaced it. So now he has a nurse he sees once every 3 months, no crisis support access and if he is ill ONLY the police and hospital. Why cant he be supported with the dignity he deserves. He cant ask for help so it is up to us but no one listens.
resp_text: Dear elephantrs85  rnWe are sorry to read about your experience and understand the immense responsibility and difficulties which often accompanies caring for people with mental health difficulties.  rnThe Crisis Resolution Teams aim is to work collaboratively with a service user’s social network in order to provide the least restrictive option for treatment in the community.  rnThe Camden and Islington Crisis Resolution Teams operate a Crisis Single Point of Access (C-SPA) service Monday to Sunday, 365 days per year between 8am and 8:45pm. This is staffed by crisis team clinicians, not administrators so that service users and carers can get to speak to a health professional directly. It is a busy part of the service and sometimes it means there is a wait to get through, hence why a voicemail option was introduced to alleviate this.  rnDuring the day in the C-SPA, there are up to six clinicians at peak times responding to calls. At night time, the service is run by a single staff member per borough who can respond and who attends the various Emergency Departments within London in addition to answering calls or voice messages where they are not able to respond immediately.  rnStaff are instructed to reply to all voicemail messages as soon as they are able, although the time can vary dependent on the nature of the work. As the C-SPA is the single point of access for all the crisis teams in Camden and Islington, there are a high volume of calls received hence why at times, individuals ringing in are directed to leave a voicemail when they cannot get through.  rnThe aim of the C-SPA is to provide telephone support to callers and aims to support referrals into the Crisis Team services thus ideally preventing servicer users needing to present themselves to an Accident and Emergency department. This is achieved through providing support and advice earlier on their mental health crisis.  rnIf offered assessment with the Crisis Team services we then aim to see those in crisis within 24 hours. We acknowledge that we are not able to offer assessments any sooner than this time period currently, but this is something we are working hard on improving. At times, therefore, we may have had to explain to service users and their carers that if a more immediate and emergency type response is required, then unfortunately we would be unable to meet this.  rnOn speaking with the service provider for the Assertive Outreach Team I would like to reassure you there have not been any cuts in this service here in Camden and Islington NHS Foundation Trust. The Assertive Outreach Team continues to provide a high level of support and care management for this client group.  rnWhilst we recognise the importance of online feedback we really want to encourage service users and carers to contact us directly, each service users’ needs are unique and this enables us to provide a more specific and tailored response.  rnThank you once more for your feedback, please do get in touch and allow us to arrange a meeting to discuss the issues you raise concerning your son in more detail.  rnNonie Fennell-KellyCrisis Single Point of Access Managernonie.fennell-kelly@candi.nhs.uk

story_text:
When I first moved to Cambridge I had already been suffering from an eating disorder for over a year, with gradually worsening behaviours.  Before that I had been self harming for over a decade.  Even then I was unwilling to seek help, as I know many in similar situations are, however being pushed by friends, I made tentative contact with my gp,  who diagnosed me with depression and sent me off with a prescription for flouxetine.  

After another year in which I returned to my gp at intermittent intervals, not finding the prescription particularly useful, I eventually was referred to the adult eating disorders service.  After a wait of approximately 6 months, I had an assessment, diagnosing me with bulimia nervosa.  I then waited for 2 years on the waiting list.  With no support whatsoever in the meantime.  

When I finally as contacted again, I had a few sessions alone with a therapist, before that therapist left the service and I was transferred to a different therapist.  A few sessions after that change I was included in a new eating disorders group.  None of this particularly helped, and once in the group I had little further contact alone with the therapist.  After some 20 sessions, the group dissolved anyway, and my time allowed in the service was up.  No attempt was made to really assess me as to whether I had actually improved, and no further contact encouraged after discharge.  Any attempt by me to say that I hadn't improved was met with the comment that many people don't actually improve until after the therapy has finished.  Which felt rather like a 'so what? you'll be out of our hair'.
resp_text: Thank you for your feedback.  I was really concerned to hear of your experiences and would really like it if you could contact me directly so we can work with you to help move things forward. rnrnWe are also going to come back with a fuller response to your feedback, once we have looked into the issues you raise in more detail.rn rnMy contact details are: Marie McKearney-PALs Team 0800 3760775rnrnSincerelyrnrnMarie-Pals Team

title_text: why is the surgery only open am
story_text: on several occasions I have tried to phone after approx. 1.30am to be told by the answer phone that the surgery is now closed why is this specifically all afternoon on the 21june I phoned in at 9 and a doctor phoned me back approx. 10. 30 telling me that they would send a prescription to boots at roundswell it never arrived in fact they said they were receiving prescription for people they shouldn't  have received they also stated thet they had tried to phone the surgery all afternoon with no joy at all why is this,it now 7 pm and I haven't received my prescription why !!!!!!
resp_text:
Thank you for your comments, all surgeries are closed on a regular basis with the authority of NHS England for a variety of reasons.
Once a quarter all surgeries within North Devon are closed for GP training sessions.
Those practices that opt to remain open over lunchtime, again with the authority of NHS England are allowed to close for all staff meetings or training sessions.
As you will know, Queens Medical Centre is taking over the Boutport Medical Centre patient list and the surgery was closed yesterday in order that a joint staff training session could be held.
The practices both followed due procedure and submitted the relevant forms to NHS England for their permission.
It is impossible to provide staff training during the day when it is always incredibly busy.
You will note that the closure was advertised on the website and there had been posters on the doors for the Surgery.
I apologise for the problems you have had with your prescription and as this feedback is anonymous, please could you contact the practice so that we can deal with this separately.
All electronic prescriptions were sent as normal and we have not been made aware of any issues today.

title_text: Great care from nurse at lymphoedema clinic
story_text:
Jan, the nurse running the Bromley Healthcare Lymphoedema Service, had a follow-up appointment with me two days ago to remeasure for compression stockings and address concerns.  
She took a lot of time, was extremely helpful and friendly, and demonstrated a lot of knowledge and expertise. I'm looking forward to the arrival of my new stockings to see how well they improve my treatment.
resp_text: Dear Sandy M,  rnThank you for taking the time to provide feedback about your positive experience of the Lymphoedema Service.  rnI am pleased to hear that Jan was very helpful and I hope the stockings arrive soon.  rnI have forwarded your comment to Jan, who I am sure will appreciate your kind words.  rnWith best wishes, Sharon  rnSharon Cranfield, Patient Experience Lead, Safer Care Team

story_text: I have been a most satisfied patient of the Riverbank Medical Centre over the four and a half years I have lived in Weston-super-Mare. I think it is extremely well run and all the staff are most helpful and courteous. I have always received the best attention whenever I have visited or had to phone in. I think, over the years I was a patient, before moving away, I have either seen or spoken with most of the doctors at some point. My opinion is that you have a wonderful team of doctors; highly motivated, skilled and with wonderful interpersonal skills - what is often called "bedside manner." The nursing staff maintain very high standards without losing the personal touch and displaying great interest in their patients. The receptionists and admin staff have always been most helpful. There is not a trace, here, of the obstructive receptionist keeping patients at a distance from the doctors. Please pass on my sincerest thanks to all at the Medical Centre for looking after me so well. You all surpassed yourselves in helping and supporting me, as a patient, in gaining more control and responsibility for my own health and well being and swiftly coming to my aid when things were beyond my control. You have the utmost respect from this former patient, and I hope that you enjoy the same respect from your current patients, too; certainly you deserve it. From a patient who is sorry to be losing you; I wish I could take you all with me.
resp_text: Thank you for your feedback. All our clinicians and staff are very caring and strive to provide the best service they can to our patients. It's always so good to hear of particular examples and I thank you for taking the time to put this on the NHS website.

title_text: My care on the hospital is excellent
story_text: My care in the hospital has been very good and I have been very impressed with the care that I have received on the ward. The nurses that I have had the pleasure of coming into contact with have been fantastic and I have been very impressed with the care that they have given me on the ward. The doctors are very happy with everyone I have seen as they have all given me a fantastic level of care and have helped me understand everything they have spoken to me about. The food has been very good and I have enjoyed the portions. I Have had no issues with my care and thank all the staff for my care
resp_text: Hello!  rnThank you for taking the time to share your feedback, it is so encouraging to learn of the excellent care you have received from the staff you have met and continue to receive. I am pleased to know you are happy with the updates you get too.  rnAll the very best in your recovery  rnstephanie

story_text:
My mum called me recently to say that she wasn't feeling well, she had said she had a bad headache, I noticed her speech was jumbled up, it wasn't slurred she was just having difficulty finding the right word and she was confused.  
Given the fact she had a stroke in January i went straight up to make sure she was OK, we decided to call NHS24. The call handler was fantastic, he listened and repeated everything back to me to make sure he was getting the right information. It was decided that given my mums previous stroke it was best to send an ambulance which we actually thought would happen.  
The ambulance arrived at my mums promptly and unfortunately this is where the level of care and compassion dropped quite dramatically. The ambulance crew came in to my mums and started checking her vital signs and asked her a few questions. I explained what had happened before their arrival and what prompted our call to NHS24. They asked me why i called NHS24, i thought it would be fairly evident but apparently not. One of the crew then questioned if had been looking for an ambulance to be sent? Asking if it wasn't serious enough to call 999, but it was serious enough to call NHS24? I explained to them that we understand they're busy especially just now and reminded them it was NHS24 that sent them. They then said they weren't questioning my motives, but there is no reason for my mum to be conveyed to hospital. I told them that my mums confusion and jumbled up speech were not normal, my niece was also there and reiterated this to them but it made no difference. I think they had already decided that she wasn't going to be going to hospital.  
They said her vitals were all normal and there was no reason to rush her to hospital. They then presented her with 3 choices, she could go to hospital (this was discouraged by the crew), they could arrange OOH GP to see her or she could wait to see her own GP in the morning (this was pushed as the preferred option by the crew). I didn't see the point in arguing with them anymore and they left, although they did say if anything worsens call them right back on 999. In all honesty we wouldn't have been calling them back to the house nor will we in the future after that particular experience. One of the crew didn't say anything out of turn they were great with my mum, it was the other staff member who made the decisions.  
We contacted my mums GP in the morning and she was told to go straight down, after carrying out a full neurological exam and knowing her history she contacted ambulance control and arranged an urgent transfer to Ninewells, the GP did say that my mum should have been seen at hospital the previous night, i'm not sure if she raised this with the ambulance service while she was on the phone to control. My mum was transferred to Ninewells by a really good crew who understood everything and her anxieties about hospitals etc. I can't fault them.  
My thoughts on this are surely every crew should treat patients and their family with compassion and respect and really shouldn't be questioning their motives when they are needing help with their relatives. I have never had anything bad to say about Scottish Ambulance Service and it is regretful that i have to raise this. We won't be calling NHS24 (purely so an ambulance won't be sent by them, no other reason) or ambulance service again, i'll convey my mum to hospital myself, that way i know she will be treated with a bit of compassion and we won't get attitude from a member of staff.  
The last time this happened, the ambulance crew were amazing, there wasn't any fault to be found. they treated my mum with kindness and compassion which is a stark contrast to what happened this time.  
My mums opinion on this is if they don't want to help people when they're clearly not well then they shouldn't be in the job. The ambulance service assist people in their darkest hours or minutes and they should be trying to ease the anxiety of needing an ambulance etc, it felt like we were being berated for them being there.  
It turns out my mum had had a stroke and spent 4 days in hospital, just because vital signs are normal doesn't mean someone isn't having a stroke. I'm glad the GP picked up on this quickly and she is back on the mend for a second time. If there is a 3rd, the ambulance service would be the last organisation we will be contacting.
resp_text: Dear JL59665,  rnI am sorry to hear of the poor experience you and your mum had on one of the occasions we attended her, I hope your mum is recovering well.  rnWe aim to deliver a caring and compassionate service for all patients and their families and it is disappointing to hear we let you and your mum down on this occasion. Please don't let this experience prevent you from calling an ambulance in future if you need to.  rnWith your permission I would like to arrange for a review to take place to allow us to learn from your feedback. If you would be agreeable to this, I would need a little more detail from you- I can be contacted directly at [kfreeburn@nhs.net](mailto:kfreeburn@nhs.net)  rnThank you again for sharing your experience, I am sorry that it wasn't to the high standard we expect.  rnKenny

title_text: My father passed away today after ...
story_text:
What I liked
My father passed away today after a serious illness in hospital.
During the time I visited him in Chichester and Grant ward I was so imppressed with the kindness and proffesionalism of all the staff including the cleaning staff who were constantly keeping the wards clean.
The staff all seemed happy in their work and this helped all my family through a very stressfull  and upseting time.

I cannot begin to thank you enougth for helping us get through the last few weeks. 

God Bless you all.
What could be improved
Nothing, my father received the best possible treatment.
Anything else?
Hand wash stations were placed all over the hospital and it was good to see  every one using them to help combat germs.
resp_text: Thank you for taking the time to give us such positive feedback at what must be an incredibly difficult time for you and your family. It is very much appreciated.

title_text: can't get appointment without doctor calling...
story_text:
Terrible service especially when you are ill and needing to see a doctor.
The policy is to ring up and then a doctor will call you and decide if you need to be seen.
They can then also apparently diagnose and treat over the phone without even seeing you in the practice.
How can you prescribe to someone you haven't done an assessment on? Reception staff do try to be helpful but understand it is difficult for them. Doctors when you do eventually see them are rude and off hand and as they have limited time don't bother to read notes properly.
I have a heart condition and haven't been called for a review for I don't know how long, slipped disc in my neck was referred to hospital and didn't get the appointment letter, what did my practice do? nothing.
I filled in a form to say why I didn't go to the hospital and that was it? no follow up nothing. My poor 83 year old mum who suffers terribly with Gout in both feet won't even go the service is so bad, she also has angina and kidney problems but suffers rather than call the pratice and never get seen.
Absolutely shocking the way patients are treat.
I have been ill for the last 2 days and have now been told by phone that my "illness" is something that I know is totally incorrect, and this is because a doctor has diagnosed me literally without even seeing me.
This could turn into something serious and they aren't bothered.
Terrible place to be registered at as they just don'e care.
resp_text: HinI appreciate you taking the time to express your concerns, If you would like to contact myself at Ibstock Surgery we can discuss your concerns in more depth.rnDiane SheasbynOperations Manager

title_text: Surprised at lack of covid precautions
story_text:
As an immunosuppressed patient I was surprised at the lack of masks lack of ventilation to protect the immunosuppressed, having read other feedback it is clear the surgery is not taking on board advice from science and data that covid is still an issue, still a risk and is air borne, ventilation and the use of Hepa filters should be a normal ‘ risk assessment’ made in all health care settings to mitigate the risk of covid to the most at risk, although not surprised given Vine surgeries overall lack of attention to its patients.

Maybe take a read of the NHS use of Hepa filters in healthcare settings
resp_text:
Dear Anonymous,
Throughout the pandemic we, like all surgeries have followed national guidance about the infection control measures that are needed to be in place to keep our patients and staff as safe as possible.
The national guidance changed in April this year, placing the responsibility on the surgery to carry out risk assessments to determine whether the wearing of masks should be mandatory or not, taking into account Covid infection levels in the local community (as well as staff absence due to respiratory conditions) as a guide.
We now offer patients the option of wearing a mask UNLESS they present with respiratory symptoms, in which case, this is mandatory for both the patient and staff member seeing them.  The same is true for our immuno-suppressed patients;  once the staff member is aware of their immuno-suppressed health status, they will routinely put on a mask to ensure the greatest degree of protection. 
I am sorry if you have not found this to be the case and would appreciate any further information you are able to provide to help me investigate this issue in more detail. Please contact me at hiowicb-hsi.vinemedicalgroup@nhs.net.
Thanks,
Sarah
Assistant Practice Manager

story_text: Had to bring my daughter to the A and E department last friday as she had had a bump to the head, the reception staff were brilliant and so was the staff who checked my daughter over, the health care assistant was very funny and this made my daughter feel relaxed and she cooperated with everything they asked her to do then after a short wait she was seen by the doctor who again was brilliant with her. All in all i could not fault each and every member of staff that dealt with us. They were a credit to this NHS and for which they should be very proud. Thank you and keep us the amazing work
resp_text: Thank you for taking the time to leave a comment on NHS Choices. We will pass your thanks and appreciation on to the team in accident and emergency. We hope your daughter is feeling better.

title_text: Four months (and counting) wait for short term assessment and recovery team (mental health)
story_text: I was referred by my GP to the Short Term Assessment and recovery Team at St Ann's mental health services in late September 2009 due to depression and anxiety and the fact that my privately paid for therapist believes that I may be bipolar II. Four months later, I'm still waiting. My GP has followed up twice and so have I, being told that they will call me back, but haven't done so. It's a good job I'm not suicidal. Having said that, my mental (and physical) health is getting worse. I could go to the walk-in, but I feel so depressed I can hardly leave the house or even get out of bed some days. I'm currently off work with physical health problem possibly brought on by feeling exhausted, run-down and depressed. Maybe I have to go completely crazy before anyone will help me?
resp_text:
We are sorry that your appointment has been delayed. Our START service has been experiencing high numbers of referrals in recent months and is working hard to reduce response times to referrers and service users.

We will integrate START and the 24/7 Emergency Reception Centre (ERC) from March.  By June 2010 START will offer first appointments within five working days from receipt of referral.  In the meantime, service users who require a more immediate assessment of their mental health needs can continue to self-refer directly to the 24/7 ERC at St Ann’s Hospital, where an appointment is not necessary.

Patient Opinion is an anonymised service.  If you would like us to look into your case, please contact the Trust PALS service who will be available to look into your concerns.
PALS tel: 020 8442 5427
PALS email: PALS@beh-mht.nhs.uk
Opening hours: 9.00am to 5.00pm, Monday to Friday

To find out more about how PALS can help, please visit our website: www.beh-mht.nhs.uk/pals.

story_text: Spent over two hours on the 'phone two separate occasions today trying to get through. Arranged a call back 'after 3.00' as I was working. My 'phone rang at exactly 3.00 and I did not get to it in time. They did not try again. I've waited over two hours for them today, and they couldn't wait for one minute for me. I tried to call back at 4.45 only to be told the practice is closed. I was advised by a friend who is a Consultant to see the GP because of possible blood clots following an Astra Zeneca vaccination, so really could have done with some reassurance. The GPs, once you see them, are generally good, but the access at this practice is totally inadequate. You don't even get told where you are in the queue now, just that there are 'numerous' people answering the 'phone. I waited 70 minutes this morning to get through and another 40 minutes from 3.01. I gave up in the end. I cannot believe that this practice has been graded 'Outstanding' by QCQ.
resp_text:
Apologies for your issues Dr Nock.  I believe my colleague has been in touch to try and resolve your issues.

LH.

story_text:
I moved to Kensington and Chelsea area at the end of January, and was discharged from my previous community mental health hub at the end of April once my therapy sessions ended.  
Unfortunately this has now resulted in me being in receipt of absolutely zero support for the last 4 and a half months, in which time my mental health health has been left to significantly worsen along with increasing suicidal thoughts.  
Although I had an assessment with the South Kensington team in July, unfortunately it became clear to me after the assessment that the outcome had seemingly already been pre-decided before the assessment had taken place, rendering the assessment worthless in my opinion. It seems to me that the assessment was carried out purely for performative purposes, looking on paper as though something was being done, when in reality there seemed to be little interest or intention whatsoever in helping me. They did nothing. I was simply left a dreadful website, which mainly just listed private therapy providers (none of which I can afford) along with other absolutely useless suggestions for services that I have found abysmal in the past.  
My GP has put in several referrals even since the assessment, but the South Kensington team turn down every single referral falsely claiming there is 'no change in presentation'. It seems that they will continue to refuse all and every referral, perhaps resulting in breaking my will to continue instead of offering any support.  
Unfortunately I don't believe I was ever going to be assessed with an open mind, I am sure the outcome was already decided before the appointment had even taken place. After which time they simply said to go away and try again in 3 months. I am becoming increasingly suicidal by the day and my mental state has significantly worsened and severely deteriorated. I don't have any kind of support network after years of restrictions. This makes the lack of help and support even more terrifying, and I spend my days feeling extremely frightened, vulnerable, and in floods of tears daily.  
It's often said that these type of services aim to support people to avoid hospital admissions. But at the rate it's going it's extremely likely I would end up as an inpatient again, since no support whatsoever has been offered. If someone with severe suicidal thoughts is left without support in the community with no intervention then it only ends up creating more burden on the NHS when they are so unable to cope alone that they end up back in hospital.  
I don't want to make a complaint, as that is a totally pointless exercise. neither do I want another meaningless generic standardised response from PALS as a reply to me sharing my experience. Because for me we are way past that now.  
I want this to be seen by all of the staff at the South Kensington and Chelsea mental health team. This isn't going to go away.
resp_text:
Sorry to hear of your experience and would welcome an opportunity for you to meet with the Team / Service manager to discuss your experiences. You can contact the service directly on 0203 315 3577 or via email: southkcrt.cnwl@nhs.net. The service operates between 9am-5pm Monday to Friday.  
Should you find yourself needing mental health support you can contact our Single Point of Access 24 hours per day, 7 days a week on 0800 0234 650. The single point of access is a telephone service that provides information, advice, referrals and crisis support.

title_text: Fantastic staff at Perth Royal Infirmary
story_text:
A massive thank you to the fantastic team in ward 7, and theatre staff in Perth Royal Infirmary.  
The care and compassion I have received has been truly wonderful. The staff are so kind, professional and respected my dignity at all times. The teams worked so seamlessly and all care explained to me. Both departments were calm with a very positive atmosphere.  
The staff showed great support with the sip till send initiative. Interestingly the anaesthetist explained a procedure which included sensors on my forehead to monitor brain activity during the procedure to ensure that I got just enough anaesthetic for me. They used an analogy of Goldilocks and the three bears “not to little, not too much…just enough”. I woke up feeling so much better than I did the last time I had a general anaesthetic a month ago.  
The environment and equipment was clean and well maintained. I observed the staff wearing PPE and performing good hand hygiene.  
I do think there could be more vital signs machines, I think it would allow the staff to work more effectively.  
Thank you!
resp_text: Dear still_smiling,  rnThank you so much for taking the time to give your feedback on your recent surgery in PRI. I am so glad that you had such a positive experience with the wonderful teams that we have in theatre and in the ward.  rnIt is good to know that the analogies used were helpful to you, and that everything was explained clearly. It is reassuring to hear of the good practice that you observed being carried. The Sip Til Send project is one we are really proud of, and which benefits all our patients who come to theatre. I will share your message with the team.  rnI hope you are continuing to recover after your surgery and that you are keeping well.  rnTake Care,  rnGillian Birrell, Senior Nurse, Theatres & Anaesthesia, NHS Tayside

Anything less than welcoming should never be allowed to happen and I will pass this on to the team for them to reflect on this. I would not want your experience to affect your health.rnWe would encourage any patients experiencing a less than satisfactory service to contact our Patient Support Service on 0300 013 4799 to discuss this or at: feedback.cnwl@nhs.net.rnDenise RaynernService Manager

story_text:
Since my daughter has been about 10 she has been in & out of counselling due to stress, depression,  anxiety dealing with fibromyalgia & other ailments. Over the last few months she has been to her gp several times, Lift & the urgent care team.. No one seems to help, very black & white, read this sheet & do breathing exercises. This came to a head last earlier this week when she ended up walking up a dual carriage way into incoming traffic.. she was taken to sandalwood for her safety, assessed & told she would be assigned a care worker. She was let out the next day. A care worker came round once, filled out the forms, ticked a few boxes & off she went. Daughter got really down yesterday called the urgent care team again & her gp & sandalwood, yet again total waste of time. Urgent care team.. More like couldn't care team.
My daughter just wants to see a counsellor on a regular basis to help work through things & sort herself out but keeps getting told, we dont do that.. try here, try there & pushed from pillar to post. You mean to tell me there are no adult counsellors in the swindon area! The whole system is a complete joke. Does she have get severly hurt before anyone will help?
resp_text: I am sorry to read that your daughter has been unwell lately and that you do not feel that services have helped. We would like to look into this for you and would be grateful if you could contact the PALS team on 01249 468261 or 0800 073 1778 or by email at awp.pals@nhs.net

title_text: A concern after self checking my breasts.
story_text: I had breast pain and an abnormality which concerned me, I completed an e-consult online form for my GP surgery. Within 24hrs I had spoken to a GP, I was seen the next day. Within 2 weeks I had an appointment for the Pilgrim Breast clinic. This is a multi team of doctors, nurses, radiographers and consultants.   The staff were amazing, I saw a consultant, had a mammogram,   had an ultrasound and saw the consultant again before being discharged with the happy knowledge that I was a lucky lady and all was clear for me. I am so grateful to have access to such a fantastic service. I cannot fault the professional, kind, caring and thoughtful team who were part of my care.
resp_text: Dear Salbert  rnThank you for your feedback.  rnIt's very pleasing to hear your experience with the team at Holbeach Medical Centre and Pilgrim Hospital was a positive one at a what must have been a very worrying time for you. It sounds as though the care you were provided was very prompt and you were seen and test were completed very fast enabling your mind was put at rest.  rnI am delighted to hear that everything came back clear for you.  rnThank you for taking the time to comment on the service you have received  rnWith best wishes.  rnSophie Crofts  rnPractice Manager

story_text:
This Gp practice seems to still be in the 70s/80s. I've had the Gp trying to discuss a point to myself about a pharmacist not wanting to release an item due to concerns on allergies and it was unprofessional of the Gp to try and convince me that the pharmacist was in the wrong (it's the pharmacy next to them), they should've had this discussion with the pharmacist not the patient.
They have a new person working on reception who is incredibly rude and made false accusations against myself saying I was shouting at them, when I actually was being stern due to the ongoing problem that's been going on for over a week and was still done wrong due to not documentation these correctly. I only raised my voice after this person accused me of swearing. They need training on what words are actually swear words. I believe this is not a one-off occurrence as I have heard other people who are patients at this Gp practice who have similar run-ins with this person. 
The Gp's believe that babies and young children do not have seizures due to high fevers, which is absolute nonsense. 
There is no queueing system for the phone line, so you have no choice but to continuously call until you finally get through and then most of the time you'll get put on hold.
Gp's are very old fashion and can be very condescending. Even stated some conditions to be and I quote "flavour of the month". One of the Gp's swore in front of my then toddler.
There are no female doctors. One of the nurses was also insensitive towards myself when I went in for my smear. Unfortunately it's very painful for me (probably due to having endometriosis) and the nurse made me feel like I was being dramatic.
One of the receptionists though is an absolute angel and so is the other nurse.
I can no longer deal with the issues that have been ongoing and as soon as I have everything sorted out, I will have to move Gp practices. I have too many health problems that I have to deal with to then also have to deal with this.
resp_text:
Dear patient,
Thank you for your feedback. We are sorry you have had a poor experience at the practice. If you would like to contact me to discuss these issues so they can be investigated.
kind regards
Lisa Evans
Practice manager

title_text: Staff were angels; ward was uncomfortably warm"
story_text:
My time in Daisy Hill has been good so far. I came in for a planned caesarean section which went really well. All the staff involved in the C-section were excellent. They managed to keep me calm and relaxed - to a certain extent! I honestly could not fault the team. I was kept at ease throughout - they really were all angels.  
Up on the Maternity Ward it has also been great. Everyone has been brilliant and ever so helpful.  
There is one staff member I would like to particularly give a mention to and that is Geraldine. Geraldine was with me for my whole first day in hospital. She really made me feel so so relaxed - absolutely great. I can only describe her as being an angel on earth.  
The only complaint I would have about my stay here up on Maternity would be the heat on the ward. It has just been so uncomfortably warm - even at night - which has made it incredibly difficult to sleep.  
Other than this one minor issue, my stay has been great and I would like to thank everyone who has looked after me!

Thank you so much for telling us about your experience here on the ward.

I will certainly pass on your lovely comments about Geraldine to her - it is lovely that staff get to hear positive feedback and to know that they are making a difference to the women and babies in their care.  
I would like to take this opportunity to wish you and your family well for the future.

story_text: I know this is not an A&E department but I was unhappy that it wasn't open on Saturday morning when my brother needed to go my brother had fallen off his scooter the day before and he had badly hurt it we went to grandmas house for the morning as she lives in Wimborne we took him to this hospital and I was cross that it wasn't open at 11:00 on Saturday morning I understand that it can't be open on Sundays but really it needs to be open on Saturday at least in the morning    We then decided that it wasn't worth a trip to poole or bournemouth but he did need some medical attention as he was in bad pain but I am surprised that this hospital isn't open on a Saturday morning
resp_text: “Thank you for visiting NHS Choices to leave feedback about your experience of trying to see a nurse or doctor in Wimborne with your brother.  I’m sorry that our hospital was not open to help you on this occasion.  If you ever find yourself in that position again, please call 111 from any phone.  You can call 111 when you need medical help fast but it’s not a 999 emergency or a life-threatening situation.  You can find out more about the 111 service at: http://www.nhs.uk/NHSEngland/AboutNHSservices/Emergencyandurgentcareservices/Pages/NHS-111.asp  “Call 111 if:  • you need medical help fast but it's not a 999 emergency   • you think you need to go to A&E or need another NHS urgent care service   • you don't know who to call or you don't have a GP to call   • you need health information or reassurance about what to do next   “In terms of the opening hours of Wimborne hospital, we are always monitoring how many people are using the hospital and at present, we have agreed with local GPs that for now, we do not open at weekends. But we will continue to review that decision and will talk again to local GPs about whether or not we need to open the hospital.”

title_text: Patients for over 25 Yr now never felt less…
story_text:
This new system (replacing econsult which worked for my family) just seems ridiculous! Unless you have the power to predict ill health in advance - literally no chance seeing a gp sadly during 1st week 
of illness. Appointments  over a week away sadly.  The system said the surgery should hold urgent appointments back for on the day if you meet the requirements following online consult?  Well I was told on ringing the surgery -they had NO such appointments. So I ended up dealing with 111. My condition got worse, rang the surgery again (as I am disabled so can only access online services when I have support) I explained situation to the receptionist who agreed to get me a call back. I was called back and told I needed to be seen! Exactly as I knew & had been trying to access! HOWEVER the only option was the on site paramedic who didn't even have an adult blood pressure cuff! He  advised me if I still felt ill the next day I may need to change the current antibiotics. Next day, I'm worse - I tried contacting surgery AGAIN online  to get a prescription change - totally useless as it wanted me to make another appointment over a week away!! In the end I went to Erith hospital where I was told I would face a 3hour plus wait - only to be triaged and taken IMMEDIATELY  through for treatment as my condition had worsened as I was well aware!! An hour  & a half later following prioritised treatment & observation I was allowed home with a new prescription too! Well done Erith hospital and good job I KNEW my condition was getting worse so to go for help as accessing Belvedere Medical Centre and a gp when needed was impossible sadly! Luckily I took a leap of faith well over a week before when I first started feeling unwell & booked one of these joke 'over a week ahead appointments ' as I now need  gp review according to the hospital next week. I thought we were supposed to try & leave hospital care for those with emergencies? Which had a gp been able to see me in that 1st week of getting ill i may not out of desperation (& emergency I) ended up a patient needing immediate hosp care! It's just not good enough Belvedere Medical Centre- and I'm so sad that after being with this practice for over 25 years I have NEVER KNOWN THINGS SO BAD!! People need to see or speak to a gp preferably in that first week of being ill - not over a week later etc and booking appointments that are only on offer for over a week away... This new system will cause many issues as far as I can see.  As a disabled patient I don't feel very safe currently with the care being offered.. It's not the gp themselves - it's just trying to access basic gp care that should be an option within the 1st week if serious - and saving patients from trauma & upset of them ending up at the hospital just to get the treatment they then require as the initial gp care wasn't accessible. Did this practice grow too big?? Because there weren't issues like this years ago that's all I can say...very concerning & sad
resp_text:
We do apologise for your recent experience with Belvedere Medical Centre.
We would encourage you to make yourself known to the practice so that we can look into at the details of your consultation.  
If you would like to discuss this further or have any specific concerns please make an appointment with the Practice Manager

title_text: Awful experience during admission to gynaecology
story_text:
If someone were to ask me to use one word to describe Ward 49 during my admission, it would be a circus.  
I was admitted with severe pelvic pain and bleeding from all exits.  
Firstly, I had to remind them repeatedly that I'm allergic to ibuprofen as well as being asthmatic. Despite this, I was constantly given this medication in a tub. I also have a wheat and milk allergy and had to constantly educate your staff on what gluten free and dairy free meant. This is the most basic of healthcare, can we please have our healthcare staff educated in serious allergies?  
Secondly, medical notes from years ago were being used despite other admissions and healthcare since then. I found this to be unnecessary. Not to mention the constant ward rounds with multiple different doctors switching and changing care plans. The number of times I was stopped and started on antibiotics, told I would be getting certain pain medication then not, arranging scans then canceling them, missing dosage times of meds because of what I believe was complete incompetence, I found shameful.  
I had no idea what time I was given medication, or what, as they were so frequently changed. I checked my discharge notes which stated I was given tramadol without my consent or knowledge. As far as I knew, I was given co-codamol as pain medication for the duration of my care. I believe there is an over-emphasis on administration, with staff not understanding that behind those notes is a real, living, breathing human being, and likely suffering. We are not tick-box exercises for staff to keep swapping and changing the plan multiple times a day without discussing this with the patient or getting the patient's consent. There should be someone keeping order in there, there didn't appear to be a hierarchy/no one leading things like patient care, patient needs, etc. There is no one for patients to approach to highlight these issues and concerns in real-time.  
Thirdly, the attitude of one nurse, who shouted at me and walked away from me whilst slamming the door shut on me I found disgusting. I simply asked for the name of the doctor I spoke to, for future reference as they were from a different department, and I wanted a second opinion. They replied that theydon’t have time for this nonsense, I’m on my way home. I replied asking if they would be kind enough to ask a colleague if they could give me their name as they were leaving. The nurse loudly sighed stating that they don’t have time for this sh*t, man, and proceeded to march away and slammed the room door. This caused me distress and alarm, especially the next morning when they returned for the morning rounds. I felt bullied and attacked as they said good morning to the others and not myself, making me feel like a target for asking a simple request the night before. There is very much a high school bully vibe there.  
Further, hospital wards are to rest when sick and to hopefully get well. Some, not all, staff in that ward, I felt, treated it like it was a holiday resort in Benidorm. I understand the need for staff morale and to keep a positive work environment; that is something that should be done away from patients in a staff-only area. Blasting Whitney Houston from the Nurses station radio, singing I want to dance with somebody, at the top of your lungs at 3 AM in a hospital ward, is not it. Particularly when there are patients who are distressed, crying, and feeling unsafe.  
Another issue was the lack of cleanliness in the room. The toilet and shower were shared areas and the toilet was constantly soiled with blood and fecal matter. The first shower area was unclean, with used towels laying all over the bins, and dirty cleaning products. For the bathroom, I requested it be cleaned due to infection risk and was told it would be done. It wasn’t touched the entire 3 days I was there. I requested domestic wipes and cleaned it myself. I cleaned the toilet bowl and seat whilst being doubled over in pain but it was the only way to sit and provide urine samples without being mixed with other people’s bodily fluids.  
Another thing that I found shocking was overhearing the staff discuss the need for beds, and briefing everyone by saying, Right folks, let's get these beds emptied. I don't find that professional or reassuring, and it definitely made me feel uneasy and a burden as a patient in need of care, particularly as I was rushed home without proper investigation or treatment.  
Lastly, and most importantly, I was discharged with active bleeding and pain. The source of the bleeding was not found, and no tests were arranged even as an outpatient. I was, again, given Ibuprofen as meds to take at home, despite saying repeatedly, that I’m allergic to it and asthmatic. I was also referred to a pain clinic against my wishes, as it is not applicable in my case as this is acute pain. I really don’t know what on earth was going on in that place, but it was like something I’d never seen before. I will never go back to that ward, even on my deathbed. What an absolute disgrace to the NHS.  
Note, there were three nurses that were extremely kind and compassionate and did what they could to help me. There was also one doctor who was extremely kind and took the time to take a detailed explanation of my issues, who created a care plan and was empathetic. Sadly, that care plan was again changed by someone I did not know, so I did not get the benefit of this.  
All in all, I think this department needs a major investigation into how it’s operated and to make changes where necessary.  
Absolutely horrific.
resp_text:
Dear mariak  
I am very sorry to read about your experience in Ward 49 at the Queen Elizabeth University Hospital.  
Please be reassured I will fully investigate all of the issues you have highlighted. Please can you contact me directly at Isabel.traynor@ggc.scot.nhs.uk with your details?  
Best wishes  
Isabel Traynor  
Lead Nurse, Gynaecology
